items.front: What item comes immediately after aircraft identification in the listed clearance-item order?
When the clearance limit is an airport, what must follow the airport name?
When the clearance limit is a NAVAID and the type is known, what must follow the name?
When the clearance limit is an intersection or waypoint and the type is known, what must follow the name?
items.back: The type of intersection or waypoint.

items.front: What item is listed last in the clearance-item order?
With an altitude assignment to an airborne USAF aircraft, how many of the listed extra items may be included in the same transmission?
How is a relayed clearance, advisory, or request prefaced through a non-ATC facility?
items.back: With the appropriate phrase such as ATC clears, ATC advises, or ATC requests.

items.front: Which organizations are specifically required to use the appropriate ATC prefix when relaying clearances?
items.back: Flight service stations and ARTCC Flight Data Units.

items.front: Does the prefix requirement apply only to relayed clearances?
items.back: No. It also applies to relayed information and requests for information.

items.front: What is one approved relay prefix?
What should you do if specific clearance delivery instructions are appropriate?
items.back: Issue the specific clearance delivery instructions.

items.front: What circumstance triggers the rule in paragraph 4-2-3?
items.back: When specific clearance delivery instructions are appropriate.

items.front: What is the complete rule in paragraph 4-2-3?
items.back: Issue specific clearance delivery instructions, if appropriate.

items.front: What should not be done if needed delivery instructions are appropriate?
items.back: Do not omit them and expect the pilot to infer them.

items.front: How must a clearance be relayed?
May a relayed clearance be paraphrased if the meaning seems unchanged?
What is the full rule in paragraph 4-2-4?
What is not permitted when relaying a clearance?
What is one approved way to amend a previously issued route clearance?
items.back: State the route amendment and then say the rest of the route is unchanged.

items.front: What phraseology may be used to amend a route by identifying the portion being changed?
items.back: Change (portion of route) to read (new portion of route).

items.front: Does a direct clearance to a point on the previously issued route require the phrase rest of route unchanged?
items.back: No, though it may be used if needed to clarify where the route resumes.

items.front: When route or altitude in a previously issued clearance is amended, what must be restated?
What happens if altitude to maintain is changed or restated and previously issued altitude restrictions are omitted?
items.back: Those previously issued altitude restrictions are canceled, including SID or STAR altitude restrictions if any.

items.front: Can ATC cancel or amend crossing altitudes and speed restrictions on obstacle departure procedures?
What must be done if a speed restriction is declined because it cannot be complied with concurrently with a previously issued altitude restriction?
What phrase is not a valid substitute for an amended clearance with altitude or speed restrictions?
